mnemonics.title: Metacharacter Meanings
mnemonics.content: Think of + as '1 or more' (add one), ? as 'optional' (question mark = maybe), * as 'wildcard' (many possibilities), . as 'any' (dot = anything)

mnemonics.title: Anchors Remember
mnemonics.content: ^ is at the START of the word (caret looks like the top of a line), $ is at the END (dollar signs end things)

mnemonics.title: Character Classes
mnemonics.content: [abc] = any of these, [^abc] = NOT any of these (^ inside means negation), [a-z] = range from a to z

acronyms.usage: Used for searching with extended regex support
The regex flavor that egrep uses with +, ?, |, (), {} metacharacters
Standard that defines [[:alpha:]], [[:digit:]] etc. character classes

visualTips.visualization: Think of | as an OR gate - either path can be taken. It divides patterns into alternatives that are tried from left to right.

visualTips.visualize: Think of braces as specifying minimum and maximum quantity. {2,5} means at least 2 but no more than 5 occurrences.
Square brackets create a box - the character must fit inside this box to match. Negation [^] puts a NOT sign on the box.

analogies.analogy: + is like having at least one item in your shopping cart, * is like an empty cart is also okay (optional).
Backreferences \1, \2 are like memory slots - they remember what a group matched and let you match the same thing again.
\b is like a wall between words - it stops at the edge where a word begins or ends.
